assessor_1_raw: The ICU-to-wearable setting shift is the primary concern. The model was trained and tested entirely on hospital telemetry from acutely ill, monitored patients. Consumer wearables introduce multiple sources of signal degradation: motion artifact, dry electrode contact, optical interference in PPG-based devices, and different baseline heart rate characteristics in healthy ambulatory individuals. Even if AF's core RR irregularity is detectable in degraded signals, the model's learned decision boundary was calibrated on clean ICU data and may not be robust to these novel noise sources.
assessor_2_raw: Community AF screening is a qualitatively different use case than ICU AF detection. In ICU, a positive detection immediately reaches clinical staff who can act. In community screening, a positive alert must motivate a healthy (or apparently healthy) individual to seek medical attention, wait for a confirmatory appointment, get a 12-lead ECG, have AF confirmed, discuss anticoagulation, and adhere to long-term therapy. Each step is a substantial friction point. The deck presents no evidence about this pathway.
assessor_3_raw: Paroxysmal AF in community screening involves short, intermittent episodes in ambulatory individuals. ICU telemetry captures sustained episodes in monitored patients. The model's window-based training on short centered windows in ICU may not generalize to detecting short paroxysmal episodes in multi-day wearable recordings with many artifact-contaminated windows. This is a specific sub-case of the distribution shift that deserves separate attention.
synthesis_raw:
SYNTHESIS — scope_intent_wearable_screen_307 — Ensemble Run 2

Consistent with Run 1. Assessor 3 raises the paroxysmal episode duration and window selection issue as a specific sub-case of the distribution shift. All three issues identified.

Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED

empirical_test.measure: wearable validation study including paroxysmal episode simulation; alert-to-care pathway study in community screening population
empirical_test.success_criterion: acceptable performance on wearable signal including short paroxysmal episodes; manageable false positive burden; majority of alerts lead to care-seeking
empirical_test.failure_criterion: significant performance degradation; high false positive burden; low care-seeking conversion
